ground_truth.title: Best live TV streaming service: The top 5 picks for entertainment, sports, and more
ground_truth.author: Jared Newman and Sam Singleton
ground_truth.main_content:
When you're finally ready to cut the cord, shopping for a live TV streaming service is an obvious starting point. They offer many of the same channels you had with cable or satellite TV, but deliver them over the internet to your smart TV or streaming device. All that said, live TV streaming services have been getting more and more expensive, and in some ways they won't be exactly like what you had with cable or satellite. Our top picks will help you avoid overpaying and get the TV channels you care about the most.

YouTube TV is the best TV streaming service overall, offering a simple interface that makes live TV easy to navigate, an unlimited DVR with a huge on-demand library (with skippable ads), and a broad selection of local, news, sports, and entertainment channels. At $83 per month, it's not exactly cheap but offers a broad mix of local stations, live news, national sports, and entertainment channels you'd get with a typical pay TV package, almost certainly at a lower price. It streams at up to 720p resolutions for most cable channels, with 1080p support for on-demand recordings.

Hulu + Live TV is the best TV streaming service runner-up, offering excellent value for Disney bundle subscribers, an expansive on-demand catalog, and recommendations and staff picks that make the app fun to explore. At $90 per month, it includes the full Disney bundle—Hulu's on-demand catalog, Disney+, and ESPN+—making it a great choice if you're already paying for Disney's streaming services.

DirecTV Stream is the best TV streaming service for sports fans, offering an excellent grid guide with useful sorting options and more regional sports networks than other services. Its pricing starts at various tiers depending on the channel package you choose.

Sling TV is the best budget-priced TV streaming service, offering the most affordable entry point into live TV streaming with Orange and Blue packages that let you customize your channel lineup.

Philo is the best budget-priced TV streaming service runner-up, offering the lowest price for a solid base package of entertainment and lifestyle channels, though it omits sports and local broadcast channels.
with: When you're finally ready to cut the cord, shopping for a live TV streaming service is an obvious starting point.
YouTube TV is the best drop-in replacement for cable or satellite TV.
Hulu + Live TV distinguishes itself by including the full Disney bundle, so you get Hulu's on-demand catalog, Disney+, and ESPN+.
DirecTV Stream offers more regional sports networks than other services.
Sling TV offers the most affordable entry point into live TV streaming with customizable channel packages.
